text: If you don't want me to watch the ball, you can get it out of the ocean.
speaker: Max Muncy
context: Dodgers infielder Max Muncy on what he told Giants pitcher Madison Bumgarner, who had angrily yelled at him to stop watching his home run and run the bases. Muncy's solo homer was the only run in a 1-0 Dodgers win in San Francisco.

source.title: Max Muncy beats Madison Bumgarner at the plate and in his post-game quotes

text: Happy? Happy is not a word that we think about in the game. You gotta think of something different. Happy, I don't know how to judge happy. We're in the middle of a contest. Nobody's happy.
speaker: Gregg Popovich
context: San Antonio Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich's deadpan reply during a mid-game sideline interview when TNT's David Aldridge asked whether he was happy with his team's shot selection against the Thunder.

source.title: Gregg Popovich rips reporter for asking if he's 'happy' about shot selection (Video)

text: Chemistry is something that you don't just throw in the frying pan and mix it up with another something and then throw it on top of something, then fry it up and put it in a tortilla put in a microwave, heat it up and it give it to you and expect it to taste good.
speaker: Kevin Garnett
context: Boston Celtics forward Kevin Garnett delivered this elaborate cooking metaphor while expressing frustration about the lack of preparation time following the 2011 NBA lockout, explaining why team chemistry can't be rushed.

source.title: Kevin Garnett's words were as entertaining as his play - ESPN Boston Celtics Blog

text: I had a cheeseburger and went to bed.
speaker: Andy Reid
context: Kansas City Chiefs head coach Andy Reid's reply, with a smile, when asked at his press conference how he celebrated the Chiefs' 35-24 AFC Championship win over the Tennessee Titans that sent them to Super Bowl LIV.

source.title: Andy Reid on how he celebrated Chiefs win: 'I had a cheeseburger and went to bed'

text: Leave me alone, I know what I'm doing!
speaker: Kimi Raikkonen
context: Formula 1 driver's deadpan team-radio reply to his Lotus race engineer Simon Rennie, who kept feeding him instructions during the 2012 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix (which Raikkonen went on to win).

source.title: F1.com — Leave me alone, I know what I'm doing: the best of Räikkönen on race radio

text: I don't think I'll be legally able to drive.
speaker: Robert MacIntyre
context: Scottish golfer MacIntyre, joking with reporters right after winning the 2024 Genesis Scottish Open, explaining he planned to celebrate so hard he might not make his next-day press conference at Royal Troon ahead of the Open Championship.

source.title: Yahoo Sports — Robert MacIntyre rallies for historic win at Scottish Open

text: What Carew does with a football, I can do with an orange.
speaker: Zlatan Ibrahimovic
context: Swedish soccer star's cocky teenage retort to fellow striker John Carew, who had dismissed Zlatan's tricks and flicks as pointless. Said in the Norwegian tabloid VG in April 2002. A classic surprising, deadpan brag.

source.title: Zlatan Ibrahimovic - Wikiquote

text: Never even liked tennis anyway.
speaker: Andy Murray
context: British tennis great and two-time Olympic singles gold medalist Murray posted this deadpan one-liner on X immediately after his final professional match

source.title: 'Never liked tennis anyway': Andy Murray shares epic post to confirm retirement from professional tennis - India TV

text: I have always felt that I needed you more than you needed me and that holds true to this very day.
speaker: Vin Scully
context: Legendary Dodgers broadcaster Vin Scully wrote a farewell letter to fans ahead of his final home series in September 2016, after 67 years calling Dodgers games.

source.title: Vin Scully writes farewell letter to fans before final Dodgers homestand (Sports Illustrated)

text: Avoid fried meats which angry up the blood. If your stomach disputes you, lie down and pacify it with cool thoughts.
speaker: Satchel Paige
context: From legendary pitcher Satchel Paige's famous 'How to Stay Young' rules, published in Collier's magazine in 1953.

source.title: Satchel Paige — Wikiquote

text: I didn't have any plates or silverware or anything, and I just started eating it with my hands.
speaker: John Madden
context: Beloved NFL coach-turned-broadcaster John Madden recounting his very first encounter with a turducken (a chicken inside a duck inside a turkey), brought to his broadcast booth during a Saints game.

source.title: John Madden made turducken a Thanksgiving football tradition (NOLA.com / The Times-Picayune)

text: Because there is always some kid who may be seeing me for the first time. I owe him my best.
speaker: Joe DiMaggio
context: Explaining why he always played so hard, even in meaningless late-season games, on the PBS American Experience profile.

source.title: PBS American Experience — Joe DiMaggio

text: I think I've heard somebody say that I was a well-dressed golfer. I guess that has something to do with the fact that a lot of people who play golf don't dress very well.
speaker: Arnold Palmer
context: A dry, self-effacing aside about his reputation as a sharp dresser, collected among Golf Monthly's best Palmer quotes.

source.title: Golf Monthly — 10 Of The Best Arnold Palmer Quotes

text: But I also know, if I was healthy, with good hips right now, I'd be the fastest 45-year-old in the country, or in the world.
speaker: Bo Jackson
context: Reflecting in retirement, years after his hip injury, in ESPN's long-form 'Bo Knows Best' feature.

source.title: ESPN E-ticket — Bo Knows Best

text: I play tennis for a living, even though I hate tennis, hate it with a dark and secret passion, and always have.
speaker: Andre Agassi
context: The startling opening confession of his memoir 'Open,' quoted in Sports Illustrated's coverage of the book.

source.title: Sports Illustrated — 'I Hate Tennis'

text: You spend a good piece of your life gripping a baseball, and in the end, it turns out that it was the other way around all the time.
speaker: Jim Bouton
context: The celebrated closing line of his tell-all baseball memoir 'Ball Four,' as quoted in the book's Wikipedia entry.

source.title: Wikipedia — Ball Four

text: Pressure is a privilege and champions adjust.
speaker: Billie Jean King
context: The line she says spontaneously came out of her mouth while motivating Lindsay Davenport as U.S. Fed Cup captain, recounted at Tennis.com.

source.title: Tennis.com — Billie Jean King shares origins of 'Pressure is a privilege'

text: Because I'm the undertaker. I've come to bury these people!
speaker: Bill Russell
context: Explaining to teammate Sam Jones why he wore a black suit to every Game 7, in his Academy of Achievement interview.

source.title: Academy of Achievement — Bill Russell Interview

text: Basketball is something that I'm just good at. But horses is something that I feel the most happiness and sadness.
speaker: Nikola Jokic
context: The Denver Nuggets center, speaking during a 2025 promotional tour at a horse ranch in Beijing, on where his real emotional highs and lows come from.

source.title: Yahoo Sports — "Basketball is just something I'm good at": Jokic's admission

text: He had that one coming for telling everybody I didn't know where Brazil was on the map.
speaker: Travis Kelce
context: The Chiefs tight end at a São Paulo press conference in September 2025, after doing an impression of teammate Patrick Mahomes as payback.

source.title: Fox News — Travis Kelce gets playful payback on Patrick Mahomes with spot-on impersonation

text: If everyone likes me, they're watching, so there has to be something there.
speaker: Tony Romo
context: The CBS lead NFL analyst, on Pat McAfee's show in January 2026, responding to criticism of his polarizing broadcasting style.

source.title: Fox News — Tony Romo addresses criticism after another up-and-down year in CBS booth

text: I'm still not really into it.
speaker: Anthony Edwards
context: The future No. 1 NBA draft pick in a 2020 ESPN feature, admitting he does not enjoy watching basketball ("To be honest, I can't watch basketball").

source.title: ESPN — The pain and promise of top NBA draft prospect Anthony Edwards

text: Resignations are for prime ministers and those caught with their trousers down, not for me
speaker: Brian Clough
context: The legendary English football manager on his refusal to quit, in a collection of his most memorable one-liners.

source.title: Sky Sports — Brian Clough in quotes: Fifteen of the legendary manager's most memorable one-liners

text: He can take his'n and beat your'n and take your'n and beat his'n.
speaker: Bum Phillips
context: The Houston Oilers head coach paying tribute to rival coach Don Shula's ability with any roster.

source.title: Sports Illustrated — Remembering Bum Phillips, the unsung defensive innovator

text: Baseball has been good to me since I quit trying to play it.
speaker: Whitey Herzog
context: The Hall of Fame manager reflecting on his so-so playing career versus his success as a skipper.

source.title: ESPN Page 2 — My 5 top quotes by Hall of Famer Whitey Herzog

text: It's like going to a bar and falling in love every year. That's what it is. You fall in love and get your heart broken, fall in love, get your heart broken.
speaker: Doc Rivers
context: The veteran NBA head coach on the recurring emotional toll of chasing a championship each season.

source.title: Andscape — Doc Rivers and the heartbreak of being an NBA head coach

text: This ain't a football game, we do this every day.
speaker: Earl Weaver
context: The Baltimore Orioles manager on the day-in, day-out grind that makes baseball unlike other sports.

source.title: CBS Sports — Eye on Quotes: The pithy brilliance of Earl Weaver

text: Coaching is a great thing, because you're working for four months then you go fish for eight.
speaker: Bob Knight
context: The Indiana basketball coach joking about the perks of the coaching profession, in a roundup of his memorable lines.

source.title: CBS Sports — Bob Knight quotes: Top 10 memorable lines from Indiana's legendary but controversial coach

text: I always look in the Sunday paper to see where Everton are in the league - starting, of course, from the bottom up.
speaker: Bill Shankly
context: The Liverpool manager needling city rivals Everton, in the club's official collection of his sayings.

source.title: Liverpool FC — Bill Shankly in his own words

text: I go with a clear conscience
speaker: Cristiano Ronaldo
context: After Portugal's World Cup exit vs Spain, 2026

source.title: Sports — ‘I go with a clear conscience’: Cristiano Ronaldo confirms he has played his las

text: Has anyone else 'completed football' like this?
speaker: Owen Bagnall
context: The Knowledge column, asking about Rodri completing every major honour

source.title: Sports — World Cup, Ballon d’Or, Champions League, Euros … has Rodri completed football?
